steps.title: Check for parallel or coincident lines
steps.content:
To quickly determine the number of solutions, we first check if these equations represent parallel lines (inconsistent system), intersecting lines (unique solution), or coincident lines (infinitely many solutions). This can be done by comparing their coefficients in standard form $ax + by = c$.
For the given system:
  • The coefficient ratios for each variable must match if the lines are coincident. The equations are 2.5x + 4.2y = 7 and 7.5x + 12.6y = 21.
steps.formula: $$\text{For } ax + by = c_1 \quad \text{and} \quad bx - ay = c_2, \text{ if } a/b = b/a = c_1/c_2, \text{ lines are coincident.}$$

steps.title: Compare coefficients to confirm solution type
steps.content:
Let's compare the ratios of the coefficients:
- Ratio for x: \(\frac{7.5}{2.5} = 3\)
- Ratio for y: \(\frac{12.6}{4.2} = 3\)
- Constant term ratio: \(\frac{21}{7} = 3\).
  • Since the ratios of coefficients and constants are equal, these lines are coincident.
steps.formula: $$\text{If } \frac{a_1}{a_2} = \frac{b_1}{b_2} = \frac{c_1}{c_2}, \text{ the system has infinitely many solutions.}$$

steps.title: Conclude based on line properties
steps.content:
Thus, the system has infinitely many solutions.
C is correct.
